volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 302 - Section 361)

sections.label: Section 302
sections.original: κείνου τε Φοίβου θʼ ἓν θέλων μαθεῖν ἔπος .
sections.translation: ’ Tis his wish to hear the self-same answer from Trophonius and Phoebus too .

sections.label: Section 303
sections.original: καρποῦ δʼ ὕπερ γῆς ἥκετʼ , ἢ παίδων πέρι ;
sections.translation: Is it to seek earth ’ s produce or fruit of offspring that ye come ?

sections.label: Section 304
sections.original: ἄπαιδές ἐσμεν , χρόνιʼ ἔχοντʼ εὐνήματα .
sections.translation: We are childless , though wedded these many years .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἔτεκες οὐδὲν πώποτʼ , ἀλλʼ ἄτεκνος εἶ ;
sections.translation: Hast thou never been a mother ? art thou wholly childless ?

sections.label: Section 306
sections.original: ὁ Φοῖβος οἶδε τὴν ἐμὴν ἀπαιδίαν .
sections.translation: Phoebus knows whether I am childless .

sections.label: Section 307
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , ὡς τἄλλʼ εὐτυχοῦσʼ οὐκ εὐτυχεῖς .
sections.translation: Unhappy wife ! how this doth mar thy fortune else so happy !

sections.label: Section 308
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἶ τίς ; ὥς σου τὴν τεκοῦσαν ὤλβισα .
sections.translation: But who art thou ? how blest I count thy mother !

sections.label: Section 309
sections.original: τοῦ θεοῦ καλοῦμαι δοῦλος εἰμί τʼ , ὦ γύναι .
sections.translation: Lady , I am called the servant of Apollo , and so I am .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: ἀνάθημα πόλεως , ἤ τινος πραθεὶς ὕπο ;
sections.translation: An offering of thy city , or sold to him by some master ?

sections.label: Section 311
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα πλὴν ἕν · Λοξίου κεκλήμεθα .
sections.translation: Naught know I but this , that I am called the slave of Loxias .

sections.label: Section 312
sections.original: ἡμεῖς σʼ ἄρʼ αὖθις , ὦ ξένʼ , ἀντοικτίρομεν .
sections.translation: Then do i in my turn pity thee , sir stranger .

sections.label: Section 313
sections.original: ὡς μὴ εἰδόθʼ ἥτις μʼ ἔτεκεν ἐξ ὅτου τʼ ἔφυν .
sections.translation: Because I know not her that bare me , or him that begat me .

sections.label: Section 314
sections.original: ναοῖσι δʼ οἰκεῖς τοισίδʼ ἢ κατὰ στέγας ;
sections.translation: Is thy home here in the temple , or hast thou a house to dwell in ?

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: ἅπαν θεοῦ μοι δῶμʼ , ἵνʼ ἂν λάβῃ μʼ ὕπνος .
sections.translation: The god ’ s whole temple is my house , wherever sleep o ’ ertakes me .

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: παῖς δʼ ὢν ἀφίκου ναὸν ἢ νεανίας ;
sections.translation: Was it as a child or young man that thou earnest to the temple ?

sections.label: Section 317
sections.original: βρέφος λέγουσιν οἱ δοκοῦντες εἰδέναι .
sections.translation: Those who seem to know the truth , say I was but a babe .

sections.label: Section 318
sections.original: καὶ τίς γάλακτί σʼ ἐξέθρεψε Δελφίδων ;
sections.translation: What Delphian maid , then , weaned thee ?

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: οὐπώποτʼ ἔγνων μαστόν · ἣ δʼ ἔθρεψέ με ⋯
sections.translation: I never knew a mother ’ s breast . But she who brought me up —

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: τίς , ὦ ταλαίπωρʼ ; ὡς νοσοῦσʼ ηὗρον νόσους .
sections.translation: Who was she , unhappy youth ? I see thy sufferings in my own .

sections.label: Section 321
sections.original: Φοίβου προφῆτις , μητέρʼ ὣς νομίζομεν .
sections.translation: The priestess of Phoebus ; I look oh her as my mother .

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: ἐς δʼ ἄνδρʼ ἀφίκου τίνα τροφὴν κεκτημένος ;
sections.translation: Until thou earnest unto man ’ s estate , what nurture hadst thou ?

sections.label: Section 323
sections.original: βωμοί μʼ ἔφερβον οὑπιών τʼ ἀεὶ ξένος .
sections.translation: The altar fed me , and the bounty of each casual guest .

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: τάλαινά σʼ ἡ τεκοῦσα · τίς ποτʼ ἦν ἄρα ;
sections.translation: Woe is thy mother , then , whoe ’ er she was !

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: ἀδίκημά του γυναικὸς ἐγενόμην ἴσως .
sections.translation: Maybe my birth was some poor woman ’ s wrong .

sections.label: Section 326
sections.original: ἔχεις δὲ βίοτον · εὖ γὰρ ἤσκησαι πέπλοις .
sections.translation: Hast thou any store , for thy dress is costly enough ?

sections.label: Section 327
sections.original: τοῖς τοῦ θεοῦ κοσμούμεθʼ , ᾧ δουλεύομεν .
sections.translation: The god I serve gives me these robes to wear .

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: οὐδʼ ᾖξας εἰς ἔρευναν ἐξευρεῖν γονάς ;
sections.translation: Wert thou never eager to inquire into thy birth ?

sections.label: Section 329
sections.original: ἔχω γὰρ οὐδέν , ὦ γύναι , τεκμήριον .
sections.translation: Ah ! yes , lady ! but I have no clue at all to guide me .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Alas ! I know another woman who hath suffered as thy mother did .

sections.label: Section 331
sections.original: τίς ; εἰ πόνου μοι ξυλλάβοι , χαίροιμεν ἄν .
sections.translation: Who is she ? If she would but help me in the task , how happy should I be !

sections.label: Section 332
sections.original: ἧς οὕνεκʼ ἦλθον δεῦρο πρὶν πόσιν μολεῖν .
sections.translation: ’ Tis she on whose account I have preceded my husband hither .

sections.label: Section 333
sections.original: ποῖόν τι χρῄζουσʼ ; ὡς ὑπουργήσω , γύναι .
sections.translation: What are thy wishes ? be sure I will serve thee , lady .

sections.label: Section 334
sections.original: μάντευμα κρυπτὸν δεομένη Φοίβου μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: I would fain obtain a secret answer from Apollo ’ s oracle .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: λέγοις ἄν · ἡμεῖς τἄλλα προξενήσομεν .
sections.translation: Name it , then ; the rest will I undertake for thee .

sections.label: Section 336
sections.original: ἄκουε δὴ τὸν μῦθον . — ἀλλʼ αἰδούμεθα .
sections.translation: Hear , then , this story . Yet am I ashamed .

sections.label: Section 337
sections.original: οὔ τἄρα πράξεις οὐδέν · ἀργὸς ἡ θεός .
sections.translation: Thus wilt thou accomplish naught , for shame is a goddess slow to act .

sections.label: Section 338
sections.original: Φοίβῳ μιγῆναί φησί τις φίλων ἐμῶν .
sections.translation: A friend of mine asserts that Phoebus lay with her .

sections.label: Section 339
sections.original: Φοίβῳ γυνὴ γεγῶσα ; μὴ λέγʼ , ὦ ξένη .
sections.translation: Phoebus with a mortal woman ? Stranger lady , say not so .

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: καὶ παῖδά γʼ ἔτεκε τῷ θεῷ λάθρα πατρός .
sections.translation: Yea , and she bare the god a child without her father ’ s knowledge .

sections.label: Section 341
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν · ἀνδρὸς ἀδικίαν αἰσχύνεται .
sections.translation: It cannot be ; some man did wrong her , and she is ashamed of it .

sections.label: Section 342
sections.original: οὔ φησιν αὐτή , καὶ πέπονθεν ἄθλια .
sections.translation: This she denieis herself ; and she hath suffered further woe .

sections.label: Section 343
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσασʼ , εἰ θεῷ συνεζύγη ;
sections.translation: How so , if she was wedded to a god ?

sections.label: Section 344
sections.original: τὸν παῖδʼ ὃν ἔτεκεν ἐξέθηκε δωμάτων .
sections.translation: The babe she bare she did expose .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: ὁ δʼ ἐκτεθεὶς παῖς ποῦ ʼστιν ; εἰσορᾷ φάος ;
sections.translation: Where is the child who was thus cast forth ? is he yet alive ?

sections.label: Section 346
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδεν οὐδείς . ταῦτα καὶ μαντεύομαι .
sections.translation: No man knoweth . That is the very thing I would ask the oracle .

sections.label: Section 347
sections.original: εἰ δʼ οὐκέτʼ ἔστι , τίνι τρόπῳ διεφθάρη ;
sections.translation: But if he be no more , how did he perish ?

sections.label: Section 348
sections.original: θῆράς σφε τὸν δύστηνον ἐλπίζει κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: She supposes that beasts devoured the hapless babe .

sections.label: Section 349
sections.original: ποίῳ τόδʼ ἔγνω χρωμένη τεκμηρίῳ ;
sections.translation: What proof led her to form this opinion ?

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: ἐλθοῦσʼ ἵνʼ αὐτὸν ἐξέθηκʼ οὐχ ηὗρʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: She came to the place where she exposed him , but found him no longer there .

sections.label: Section 351
sections.original: ἦν δὲ σταλαγμὸς ἐν στίβῳ τις αἵματος ;
sections.translation: Were any drops of blood upon the path ?

sections.label: Section 352
sections.original: οὔ φησι . καίτοι πόλλʼ ἐπεστράφη πέδον .
sections.translation: None , she says ; and yet she ranged the ground to and fro .

sections.label: Section 353
sections.original: χρόνος δὲ τίς τῷ παιδὶ διαπεπραγμένῳ ;
sections.translation: How long is it since the babe was destroyed ?

sections.label: Section 354
sections.original: σοὶ ταὐτὸν ἥβης , εἴπερ , εἶχεν ἂν μέτρον .
sections.translation: Thy age and his would measure out the self-same span , were he alive .

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: ἀδικεῖ νιν ὁ θεός · ἡ τεκοῦσα δʼ ἀθλία .
sections.translation: Hath she given birth to no other child since then ?

sections.label: Section 356
sections.original: οὔκουν ἔτʼ ἄλλον γʼ ὕστερον τίκτει γόνον .
sections.translation: The god doth wrong her , and wretched is she in having no child .

sections.label: Section 357
sections.original: τί δʼ , εἰ λάθρα νιν Φοῖβος ἐκτρέφει λαβών ;
sections.translation: But what if Phoebus privily removed her child , and is rearing it ?

sections.label: Section 358
sections.original: τὰ κοινὰ χαίρων οὐ δίκαια δρᾷ μόνος .
sections.translation: Then is he acting unfairly in keeping to himself alone a joy he ought to share .

sections.label: Section 359
sections.original: οἴμοι · προσῳδὸς ἡ τύχη τὠμῷ πάθει .
sections.translation: Ah me ! this misfortune sounds so like my own .

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: καὶ σέ , ὦ ξένʼ , οἶμαι μητέρʼ ἀθλίαν ποθεῖν .
sections.translation: Thee too , fair sir , thy poor mother misses , I am sure .

sections.label: Section 361
sections.original: καὶ μή γʼ ἐπʼ οἶκτόν μʼ ἔξαγʼ οὗ ʼλελήσμεθα .
sections.translation: Oh ! call me not back to piteous thoughts I had forgotten .
